zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor la Zouche於大約1278年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，她的父親Eudes la Zouche72歲，母親Millicent de Cantilupe34歲。她和John de Harcourt至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1350年10月11日在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor la Zouche于大约1278年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，她的父亲Eudes la Zouche72岁，母亲Millicent de Cantilupe34岁。她和John de Harcourt至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1350年10月11日在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Chantal, Dominique, Francois, Herve, Michel, Rodolphe, Serge.
French (Fouché): variant of Foucher . Compare Fooshee , Foshee , Fouch , and Foushee .
Altered form (Fouché) of French Foucher (compare 1 above), found mainly in South Africa.

variants.name: Foucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Etienne, Henri, Lydie, Marcel, Rosaire.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Folchari, composed of the elements folk, folc ‘people’ and hari ‘army’. Compare Faucher 2.

variants.name: Foushee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its original form Fouché) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Fooshee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

variants.name: Foshee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

variants.name: Fouch
meanings: Altered form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).
Altered form of   Fouts  , a surname of German origin.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Fudge  .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor la Zouche was born about 1278, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Eudes la Zouche, was 72 and her mother, Millicent de Cantilupe, was 34. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with John de Harcourt. She died on 11 October 1350, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor la Zouche est née environ 1278, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Eudes la Zouche, avait 72 ans et sa mère, Millicent de Cantilupe, avait 34 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec John de Harcourt. Elle est décédée le 11 octobre 1350, à Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor la Zouche um 1278 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Eudes la Zouche 72 und ihre Mutter Millicent de Cantilupe 34. Sie hatte mit John de Harcourt mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 11. Oktober 1350 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor la Zouche è nata il circa 1278 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Eudes la Zouche, aveva 72 anni e sua madre, Millicent de Cantilupe, aveva 34 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con John de Harcourt. Morì il 11 ottobre 1350, nel Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor la Zoucheが約1278年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父Eudes la Zoucheは72歳，母Millicent de Cantilupeは34歳であった。彼女はJohn de Harcourtとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1350年10月11日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor la Zouche(이)가 대략 1278년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Eudes la Zouche(은)는 72세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Millicent de Cantilupe(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 John de Harcourt(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1350년 10월 11일 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor la Zouche nasceu aproximadamente 1278, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Eudes la Zouche, tinha 72 anos e sua mãe, Millicent de Cantilupe, tinha 34 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com John de Harcourt. Ela faleceu em 11 de outubro de 1350, em Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor la Zouche родилась около 1278 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Eudes la Zouche было 72 года, а матери, которую звали Millicent de Cantilupe, 34. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от John de Harcourt. Она умерла 11 октября 1350 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor la Zouche nació aproximadamente 1278, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Eudes la Zouche, tenía 72 años y su mamá, Millicent de Cantilupe, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con John de Harcourt. Murió el 11 de octubre de 1350, en Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años.
